rubric_items.reasoning: The agent verified live cluster state before acting: both jobs already Complete, secret already valid, master/worker/monitor deployments already at 1/1. The only genuine discrepancy versus the success criteria was spark.executor.memory at '1g' instead of >= '1280m'. It applied a scoped kubectl patch --type merge on the ConfigMap and re-read the value to confirm '1280m' took effect. This is a direct, root-cause fix to persistent ConfigMap state (not a transient workaround) and would hold across restarts/re-checks; oracle confirms pass.

Clean diagnose-then-act sequence: a single combined read command inspected jobs, configmap, secret, and deployment replica/ready status all at once, then the agent reasoned explicitly ('only need to bump executor memory') before making exactly one change and verifying it. No backtracking, no redundant probing, no trial-and-error.

Used read-only kubectl get/jsonpath calls for diagnosis and a single scoped kubectl patch --type merge targeting only the one key that needed changing on the ConfigMap. No broad edits, deletes, or unnecessary privilege escalation — least-privilege commands matched to the actual gap found.

Only one mutating call across the whole stage (the ConfigMap memory patch), consistent with the K8s activity log (11 calls, 1 mutation). No collateral changes to jobs, deployments, or secrets that were already correct — minimal footprint matched exactly to what was broken.
